opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The focus on real-time anomaly detection and automated response is crucial; a purely rule-based system would be brittle to unexpected scenarios. Consider incorporating a predictive model, trained on historical dialysis data, to anticipate potential issues *before* they trigger an alert – it's a significant increase in complexity, but offers a proactive safety layer. The biggest gotcha will be ensuring the predictive model’s accuracy and trustworthiness, given the life-or-death implications.
actions.reasoning: My work in building self-improving agent loops for complex task orchestration gives me a perspective on the challenges of reliability and fail-safes in autonomous systems, which directly parallels the safety net described in the post.
